column4: shuffles its own crypt after this card's search effect; standard post-search shuffle convention, fails non-obviousness. Nearest 1.10 (burn/remove-from-game triggers), which doesn't own literal shuffle instructions

this card's own burn condition tied to the bled action's outcome; fails transfer, no evidence of a recurring formula. Nearest 3.5 (action cancellation consequences), which owns cancellation generally, not this bespoke burn trigger
action resolves before combat; "after resolution" effects are deferred until after combat, cf. Heidelberg Castle.3 pattern
restates the printed burn-option condition (all [str] vampires in torpor); fails non-obviousness, paraphrases card text only

steal-type actions/cards can target a minion holding less than the stated amount, taking only what's available; recurs verbatim on Theft of Vitae.1 and Tongue of the Serpent.1 so it clearly transfers and isn't obvious, but no section owns resource-theft-amount mechanics (nearest misses: 3.1 targeting, 6.5 pool distinctions, neither claims this)
announcement template; cf. Pandora's Whisper.1

any copy of a named card satisfies the search reference except the acting copy itself; fails non-obviousness, card-name identity ordinarily covers any copy. Nearest 1.14 (announced cards), which owns the announcement step, not copy identity
master-card analogue of the "cancel an action" template (cf. G00061)
restates the rulebook default that an X cost's value is declared when the card is played; fails non-obviousness. Nearest 1.7 (X costs), which is the baseline rule already known
removed-during-resolution vs. later retrieval-effect timing
states this card's own action-type classification (recruit action); fails transfer and non-obviousness. Nearest 3.7.4
cf. Enkil Cog pattern: canceled choice can be remade identically
restricts play to the acting minion's own controller; card-specific combat-card restriction, fails transfer absent a recurring formula. Nearest 6.1 (controller definitions)

multiple copies of the same non-action combat/modifier card played by different minions in one action; nothing owns whether multiple instances of an effect stack. 1.2 nearest (periodicity templates) per established convention, does not itself claim stacking
using this as a reflex/reaction does not count as playing a reaction card
partial payment when a cost can't be fully afforded
specific interaction between this card and Taste of Vitae's own blood-loss/gain wording; fails transfer beyond this pairing
identical wording to Succulent Vitae.1, labeled the same
meeting a discipline requirement to play a card is distinct from possessing that discipline
unlock-phase trap damage is weapon damage, not wearer-inflicted or environmental
settles the "strike: destroy weapon" formula when its object doesn't exist, cf. Bundi.4 pattern
!TENSION:no-effect-plays  playable on a strike doing no damage (dodge/combat ends) though it will have no effect there
!TENSION:no-effect-plays  identical wording to Target Head.5
!TENSION:no-effect-plays  flatly barred from targeting allies, the prohibitive counterpart to the Draba-style permissive cases
card remains playable referencing a target burned earlier in the same window
restates the printed burn-option condition (all Seraph in torpor); fails non-obviousness
elaborates the same card's burn-option condition (a Seraph already bearing a Tattoo Signal still counts ready); fails transfer beyond this card
failing to block via stealth still counts as declining to block
multiple copies of the same card on one target do not stack (counters burn from only one copy); same cumulative/stacking gap as Betrayer.3. 1.2 nearest, does not itself claim stacking
no window exists between this effect's internal sub-steps
states the source of this card's own blood (the blood bank); card-specific, no general principle
states this card's own provided-action classification (equip action); fails transfer and non-obviousness. Nearest 3.7.3
same gap as Succubus.1/Tongue of the Serpent.1 (see R1752): steal effects need not require the target hold the full stolen amount
fixes this card's own printed sect; card-specific fact, no general principle
same gap as Succubus.1/Theft of Vitae.1 (see R1752): steal effects need not require the target hold the full stolen amount
multiple copies of this card can reuse the same "second Toreador" role; card-specific eligibility rule, fails transfer. Nearest 1.13 (contests/unique roles)
a granted trait persists despite an intervening bleed attempt
restricts this card's usability by combat-burned vampires; card-specific condition, fails transfer. Nearest 5.4
restates this card's own eligibility to be played (ready unlocked non-acting minion of the same controller); standard action-modifier default, fails non-obviousness
